title: Save Jjs

jokes.text: Well, then you leave, Terry. And pay for the cake you already ate.
jokes.setup: Terry can't choose between wedding cakes
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact dismissal and demand for payment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope moment - her obsessive perfectionism applied to cake testing with typical bureaucratic strictness

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Terry, we don't have time for this. You're getting the first cake. I'm going to go get my checkbook.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You owe me $47.

jokes.text: That was a joke cake I got from the grocery store, to weed out low-quality palates.
jokes.setup: Someone likes the vanilla cake with lemon cream
jokes.punchline: Revelation it was a test cake to eliminate people with bad taste
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Leslie's extreme thoroughness and slightly elitist tendencies about quality

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, you actually liked that vanilla cake with lemon cream? That was a test cake to eliminate people with bad taste.

jokes.text: but I kind of like the grocery store one, too.
jokes.setup: Leslie just revealed the grocery store cake was meant to weed out bad taste
jokes.punchline: Donna immediately admits she also likes it
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that undermines Leslie's test, showing even sophisticated Donna has simple tastes

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: I like it too.

jokes.text: Butler of Honor
jokes.setup: Tom's role in Donna's wedding
jokes.punchline: Made-up title combining 'best man' and 'maid of honor'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Tom's need to make everything about himself and create new trends

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm the best maid of honor.

jokes.text: a surprise bigger than when LeBron went back to Miami
jokes.setup: Tom promises a big surprise
jokes.punchline: References LeBron going back to Miami (which never happened - he went to Cleveland then Lakers)
jokes.explanation: Tom's confused sports reference is in character but not particularly clever or accurate

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I have a huge surprise for everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: LeBron James is going back to Miami!

jokes.text: Treat Yo' Self 2017!
jokes.setup: Annual tradition being revealed
jokes.punchline: The beloved catchphrase returns
jokes.explanation: Iconic callback that fans expect and love - high impact despite low originality due to established tradition

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Every year, without fail, it happens.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Treat yo' self.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Treat yo' self.

jokes.text: I still don't know what's happening... Not an answer to my question.
jokes.setup: Terry repeatedly asks what's happening
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly frustrated as everyone just says 'Treat Yo' Self'
jokes.explanation: Good escalating frustration as the outsider who doesn't understand the tradition

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: What's happening? What is going on?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Treat yo' self!

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: No, seriously, what's happening right now?

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Treat yo' self!

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: Can someone please just tell me what is going on?!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Treat yo' self!

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: WHAT IS HAPPENING?!

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Treat yo' self.

dialogue.speaker: Terry
dialogue.line: I hate all of you!

jokes.text: We did not believe that we did anything to invade people's privacy... But then, we checked thousands of emails and texts between our customers and their loved ones, and it seemed like people thought we were wrong.
jokes.setup: Gryzzl executive denying privacy invasion
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits to checking thousands of private communications
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of tech companies' doublespeak about privacy - the contradiction is perfectly executed

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Executive
dialogue.line: We would never invade anyone's privacy.

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Executive
dialogue.line: Well, I checked 47,000 private emails this morning, but that's just routine.

jokes.text: seating is gonna be organized by income and sexual history
jokes.setup: Gryzzl promises a free concert to make amends
jokes.punchline: Reveals creepy seating arrangement based on private data
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark escalation that shows they're still using private data inappropriately even while apologizing

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Representative
dialogue.line: We'd like to make it up to you with a free concert! We've arranged special seating based on our extensive data about you.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Wait, what kind of seating arrangement?

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Representative
dialogue.line: Well, we know you like to sit alone, so we've placed you in an isolated section. We know Leslie prefers to be near the front and center of attention, so she's up there. And Roscoe, we've seated you next to people who share your exact browsing history.

dialogue.speaker: Roscoe
dialogue.line: How is that possible? That's incredibly invasive!

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Representative
dialogue.line: We know everything about you. Everything.

jokes.text: All we need is a blimp and a decoy blimp.
jokes.setup: Andy offers to help with something big and exciting
jokes.punchline: His solution involves multiple blimps for unclear reasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - unnecessarily complex and completely impractical, delivered with total confidence

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I've got it! We use multiple blimps!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Andy, why would we need multiple blimps?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I don't know, but it sounds awesome!

jokes.text: For what I hope is the last time, but we'll assume is not the last time, our strategy does not involve blimps.
jokes.setup: Andy suggests blimps again
jokes.punchline: Leslie's weary acknowledgment this is a recurring problem
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's patient exasperation with Andy's blimp obsession

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What about blimps?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Andy, we've talked about this. Multiple times. Blimps are not a viable transportation solution for our parks department.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: But they're so cool.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I know you think they're cool. That's the problem. That's always the problem.

jokes.text: I am not a spy, and I would never reveal classified information. Do you have to say that every time? Yes.
jokes.setup: Agent Walker gives his standard disclaimer
jokes.punchline: Leslie's annoyance and his inflexible commitment to protocol
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat showing bureaucratic absurdity and his rigid adherence to procedure

dialogue.speaker: Agent Walker
dialogue.line: I need to give you my standard disclaimer. Everything you tell me is classified information. You cannot share it with anyone, not your family, not your friends, not even your therapist.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Even my therapist? That seems like a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Agent Walker
dialogue.line: Especially your therapist. They're a person.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Yes, I'm aware of what a therapist is.

dialogue.speaker: Agent Walker
dialogue.line: Then you understand the risk.

jokes.text: Just like Joey Fatone and Lance Bass, we are totally in sync.
jokes.setup: Leslie describes her partnership with Ron
jokes.punchline: References two members of NSYNC, not the actual band name
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie pop culture reference that's slightly off - characteristic of her well-meaning but sometimes awkward analogies

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ron and I are like Justin Timberlake and Chris Kirkpatrick from NSYNC.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You mean the band NSYNC?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: No, I said Justin Timberlake and Chris Kirkpatrick.

jokes.text: We're in sync emotionally. Stop patting my head.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to demonstrate their synchronization
jokes.punchline: Ron is clearly uncomfortable with the physical demonstration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron/Leslie dynamic - her enthusiasm clashing with his need for personal space

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay, Ron, we're going to do a synchronized dance to show how in-sync we are as a department.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Absolutely not.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Come on, it'll be fun! Just follow my lead.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I would rather sit through a city council meeting where they discuss parking regulations for three hours.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's your favorite thing!

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Exactly.

jokes.text: Let me repeat that because it makes me very horny. $125 million.
jokes.setup: Announcing the sale price
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual response to money
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor that works for the gross character but feels somewhat cheap and obvious

dialogue.speaker: Newport Trust Representative
dialogue.line: The sale price is $47 million.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I need to go take a cold shower.

jokes.text: Do mine first. In case something happens to you, I wanna make sure I get paid. Looking out for number one. Always smart.
jokes.setup: April asking Ron to sign checks
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact admission she wants to ensure payment before he potentially dies
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - dark, practical, and delivered with characteristic deadpan confidence

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I need you to sign these checks so that if you die, I'll still get paid.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: That's... actually a very practical concern.

jokes.text: Well, breakups are famously super fun and make everyone feel awesome. So, it's no different.
jokes.setup: April asks how post-breakup life is going
jokes.punchline: Tom's sarcastic response highlighting how terrible breakups are
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response - in character but not particularly clever or original

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh, it's great. I'm sleeping on a futon in the back of my office at Entertainment 720, I'm eating nothing but protein bars and sadness, and I've started a journal where I write angry letters to my ex that I'll never send. So yeah, living the dream.

jokes.text: You should come to the wedding and do paperwork.
jokes.setup: Tom awkwardly trying to invite April to the wedding
jokes.punchline: Reducing her attendance to work duty in his discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Tom's awkwardness as he makes the invitation increasingly unromantic

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: April, I wanted to ask you something. Would you maybe want to come to my wedding? As a guest? With a plus-one if you want?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Tom, that's sweet, but I'm probably going to be pretty busy that day.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh, okay, I understand. Well, actually, we could really use some help with parking and setup, so maybe you could come as staff?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure, I can do that.

jokes.text: Stop, you're making this sound almost too fun.
jokes.setup: Tom suggesting she do paperwork at the wedding
jokes.punchline: April's dry response highlighting how unappealing he's made it
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan delivery that cuts through Tom's awkwardness with characteristic sarcasm

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Wow, Tom. You've made paperwork sound so fun. I can't believe I haven't been doing it at every social event for years.

jokes.text: I literally couldn't care less.
jokes.setup: Tom asking her to text him about the wedding decision
jokes.punchline: April claiming not to care while clearly being engaged in the conversation
jokes.explanation: Classic April defense mechanism - claiming indifference while being obviously invested

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Just text me about the wedding decision, okay?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I don't care about weddings. They're boring and pointless.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: So you won't text me?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: No, I'll text you. But only because I have nothing better to do.

jokes.text: Today is about one thing. Things. Things, Donna! Our favorite.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining what Treat Yo' Self is about
jokes.punchline: His excited emphasis on 'things' as their shared passion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment capturing Tom and Donna's materialistic bond and shared consumer enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Treat Yo' Self is about doing whatever makes you happy. For some people it's eating cupcakes, for other people it's going to the spa, but the common thread is things. We both love things.

jokes.text: Treat Yo' Self. In Beverly Hills!
jokes.setup: They've been building up to something special
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're taking the tradition to the ultimate consumer destination
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the established tradition - taking it to the logical extreme location

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Okay, so we've been perfecting this tradition for years, right?

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: The best tradition.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: And we've decided to take it to the next level. We're doing Treat Yo'self... at the mall.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: The ultimate consumer destination.

jokes.text: All I wanted was 25 square miles of land valued at $100 million given to me for free. Is that too much to ask?
jokes.setup: Leslie lamenting her failure
jokes.punchline: Framing an obviously unreasonable request as modest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her inability to see her own unreasonable expectations as anything but reasonable

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I just want something small and simple. Nothing fancy. Just a little 500-person gala with live music, a seven-course meal, and appearances by every celebrity who has ever lived.

jokes.text: I'll say it again. Voodoo is still an option.
jokes.setup: Leslie expressing frustration about losing the land
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact suggestion of supernatural revenge
jokes.explanation: Classic April - offering dark, impractical solutions with complete seriousness and persistence

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I can't believe we lost the land. This is the worst thing that's ever happened.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: You could always put a curse on them.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: April, I can't curse people.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Not with that attitude.

jokes.text: One place asked me if I wanted kale in my milkshake. My milkshake, you guys.
jokes.setup: Leslie complaining about too much kale in Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Her horror at the specific violation of milkshake sanctity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how health trends have gone too far - milkshakes represent pure indulgence being corrupted

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: There's too much kale in Pawnee. It's everywhere. And now someone's put kale in my milkshake. A milkshake! That's a violation of milkshake sanctity!

jokes.text: Even though I'm a firm believer in the free market, and your failure as a businessman is ultimately to blame, it's still a travesty your diner is closing.
jokes.setup: Ron trying to comfort JJ about his closing diner
jokes.punchline: Delivering comfort by first explaining why it's JJ's fault
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - trying to be supportive while maintaining his libertarian principles, creating awkward comfort

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: JJ, I know this is hard. But you have to understand, this is your fault.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You didn't work hard enough. You didn't manage your money well enough. And frankly, your food was mediocre at best.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: But the good news is, now you can start over. And this time, you'll fail faster.

jokes.text: Was it Putin? Voldemort Putin, of Russia? I'd love to take that bastard down.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking about JJ's landlord
jokes.punchline: Her assumption it might be a villain with a made-up middle name
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her tendency to assume the worst and create elaborate enemy narratives

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: So who's your landlord?

dialogue.speaker: JJ
dialogue.line: His name is Earl Snoodle.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Earl Snoodle? That sounds like a villain from a made-up movie.

jokes.text: Tiny rolled-up scrolls delivered by trained foxes.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks April to use whatever communication method young people use
jokes.punchline: April's completely ridiculous but delivered-seriously answer
jokes.explanation: Excellent April moment - absurdist response delivered with perfect deadpan, mocking both Leslie's question and generational stereotypes

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: April, what's the best way to reach young people? You know, use whatever communication method young people use these days.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Snapchat.

jokes.text: Oh, hell, yeah. Blimp time.
jokes.setup: Andy thinking what Leslie's thinking about their strategy
jokes.punchline: Still convinced everything involves blimps
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Andy's one-track mind about blimps - predictable but in character

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Still convinced everything involves blimps

jokes.text: I think this is a job for Jonathan Karate.
jokes.setup: Andy realizing blimp won't work
jokes.punchline: Pivoting to his serious alter ego character
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Andy's serious character - shows his ability to pivot from silly to 'serious' mode

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. A blimp? That's not gonna work.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I need to switch to my serious character. Bud Stubbins.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Bud Stubbins doesn't do blimps. Bud Stubbins does real police work.

jokes.text: I can't think of anything more noble to go to war over than bacon and eggs.
jokes.setup: Ron agreeing to help save JJ's Diner
jokes.punchline: His philosophical elevation of breakfast food to noble cause
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy - elevating simple breakfast food to noble cause with complete sincerity and meat-focused values

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Breakfast food is the foundation of civilization. Waffles, eggs, bacon — these aren't just meals, they're statements of purpose. A man who fights to save a diner is a man fighting for the soul of this community. I'm in.

jokes.text: Well, we'll keep practicing.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ron attempting some kind of synchronized action
jokes.punchline: Leslie's optimistic response to their obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing their continued lack of synchronization despite claiming to be 'in sync'

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We nailed it!

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: We did not nail it.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: But we were so close! I could feel the synergy.

jokes.text: That's not true. It's called 'constructing a narrative,' JJ. You stick to the breakfast, I'll deal with the politics.
jokes.setup: JJ correcting Leslie's false claim about building the diner himself
jokes.punchline: Leslie explaining she's deliberately creating a better story
jokes.explanation: Great meta moment showing Leslie's political instincts for narrative creation over literal truth

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I didn't build it myself. But I'm going to tell people I did.

dialogue.speaker: JJ
dialogue.line: Why would you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Because the truth is boring. 'A bunch of people worked together on a project.' Where's the narrative? Where's the hero's journey? I'm creating a better story.

jokes.text: I do not like rallies. And based on what I see from this vantage point, I do not like most of you.
jokes.setup: Ron speaking at a public rally
jokes.punchline: Opening by insulting the audience he's supposed to rally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - brutally honest and completely undermining the rally format while somehow being endearing

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I've been asked to come here today to inspire you, but I have to be honest with you. You all disgust me. You're weak, you're soft, and you're everything that's wrong with this country.

jokes.text: Please do not approach me on the street after this event and attempt to talk to me. Our similarities begin and end with this single issue.
jokes.setup: Ron finishing his rally speech
jokes.punchline: Pre-emptively shutting down any future social contact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron moment - setting clear boundaries while acknowledging shared cause, perfectly antisocial

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: And if you see me out in public, do not approach me. I will not acknowledge you. In fact, I'm going to take steps to avoid you. I'm installing a small periscope in my car so I can see over obstacles without being seen myself.

jokes.text: Exactly.
jokes.setup: Receptionist can't find 'Jonathan Karate' in appointments
jokes.punchline: Andy confirming this proves his secret identity works
jokes.explanation: Good Andy logic - treating the inability to find his fake name as proof of his secret identity's effectiveness

dialogue.speaker: Receptionist
dialogue.line: I don't have a Jonathan Karate in the appointment book.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Perfect. My secret identity is working.

jokes.text: holding in farts
jokes.setup: Andy explaining serious issues his show addresses
jokes.punchline: Including this decidedly unserious bodily function
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy humor - mixing serious topics with childish concerns in his earnest way

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Including this decidedly unserious bodily function

jokes.text: I gotta go pull the plug on my dad in 10 minutes.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying he needs to make the meeting quick
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning ending his father's life support
jokes.explanation: Effective character moment showing Dennis's callousness, though somewhat obvious shock humor

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I need to make this meeting quick. I have to go end my father's life support.

jokes.text: I'm gonna turn that place into an elbow art salon and make a killing.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his plans for JJ's Diner
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously specific and useless business concept
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd business concept that captures Dennis's shallow vanity and poor business instincts

jokes.text: Not to brag, but my colognes have been known to stunt human growth, so...
jokes.setup: Dennis warning about his cologne's effects on children
jokes.punchline: Proudly admitting his product causes physical harm
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's complete lack of safety awareness while treating it as a brag

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's probably not a good idea to wear this cologne around children. It'll make their eyes water and burn their skin.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: But that's what makes it so good!

jokes.text: you can see the house that belongs to Bruce Willis and Christina Aguilera
jokes.setup: Tour guide pointing out celebrity houses
jokes.punchline: Claiming two unrelated celebrities live together
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor playing on celebrity tour inaccuracy, but not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Tour Guide
dialogue.line: And over here on the left, you can see where Brad Pitt and Angelina Jolie live together.

jokes.text: I would like three big-ass diamonds surrounded by their own set of tiny-ass diamonds.
jokes.setup: Tom getting elbow bedazzling
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top specification for maximum bling
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment showing his commitment to excess and specific aesthetic demands

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I want you to bedazzle my elbow. But I'm talking full coverage — I want rhinestones so big and so bright that when I'm at a club, people think my elbow is a disco ball. I want my elbow to be more sparkly than a vampire in sunlight. I'm talking about a level of bling that makes Mr. T look like a minimalist.

jokes.text: LASIK for fingernails? I came up with that. You want it? It's 275.
jokes.setup: Offering various Beverly Hills services
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical service combining eye surgery with nail care
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist concept satirizing ridiculous Beverly Hills beauty treatments

dialogue.speaker: Salon Worker
dialogue.line: We offer eye surgery combined with a manicure. It's called a 'Nail-LASIK.' We remove cataracts while filing your cuticles.

jokes.text: Psych!
jokes.setup: Dennis appearing to agree to let JJ's stay
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was all a cruel fake-out
jokes.explanation: Effective misdirection showing Dennis's cruelty, though 'psych' is dated and somewhat cheap

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Of course you can stay. I'd be happy to help out an old friend.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Just kidding. No you can't. Get out.

jokes.text: you can all just sit there pulling on your puds
jokes.setup: Dennis insulting the crowd
jokes.punchline: Crude and dated insult that shows his immaturity
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that works for Dennis's character but isn't particularly clever or well-crafted

jokes.text: Give me your boos! I am nourished by your hatred!
jokes.setup: Crowd booing Dennis
jokes.punchline: His theatrical embrace of villain role
jokes.explanation: Good villain monologue that shows Dennis leaning into his villainous role with theatrical flair

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You want a villain? I'll give you a villain!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I am the villain of this story, and I embrace it fully!

jokes.text: The Hounds is an FDA-rejected cologne I've been working on that makes everything smell like wet dog.
jokes.setup: Dennis threatening to 'Release the Hounds'
jokes.punchline: It's actually a terrible cologne, not actual dogs
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific weapon that subverts expectations while being perfectly in character for Dennis

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Release the Hounds!

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It's actually a terrible cologne, not actual dogs.

jokes.text: I gotta go kill my dad.
jokes.setup: Dennis leaving the scene
jokes.punchline: Casual reminder of his earlier life support comment
jokes.explanation: Callback that reinforces Dennis's callousness but doesn't add much new comedic value

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'm gonna go now.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: But remember what I said about the life support machine.

jokes.text: Water makes it worse.
jokes.setup: Everyone trying to wash off Dennis's cologne smell
jokes.punchline: Discovery that water intensifies the horrible smell
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the cologne attack consequences, simple but effective physical comedy

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait, why does it smell worse?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh no. Water intensifies it.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Of course it does.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: We're trapped.

jokes.text: Bogdano-delish.
jokes.setup: Tom eating Peter Bogdanovich's fish
jokes.punchline: Combining the director's name with 'delicious'
jokes.explanation: Typical Tom wordplay - tries too hard but fits his character's tendency to make everything about entertainment

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: This is Bogdanovich!

jokes.text: I was secretly hoping you got me the shoes Jaden Smith wears in Hitch 2: Son of a Hitch.
jokes.setup: Tom responding to Donna's advice gift
jokes.punchline: Reference to a completely made-up sequel with ridiculous title
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Tom moment - fake movie title that sounds plausible but ridiculous, fits his pop culture obsession

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Donna, this is incredible. Did you get this from that guy who makes up movie sequels? Because I'm pretty sure I saw 'Die Hard 5: Die Hardest' advertised at the mall.

jokes.text: Treat yo' self.
jokes.setup: Donna giving relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Applying their material mantra to emotional matters
jokes.explanation: Smart application of their catchphrase to emotional territory - shows growth while maintaining brand

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Listen, you want relationship advice? It's simple. Treat yo' self.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: But that's for material things. For emotional stuff, you gotta treat the other person. Treat them with respect, honesty, and communication.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? Treat yo' self anyway. You'll need it after all those conversations.

jokes.text: Josh Groban! He's eating his own sushi! That's the most baller thing I've ever seen!
jokes.setup: Tom spotting Josh Groban at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: His excitement about the singer eating fish previously owned by himself
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the celebrity fish concept and Tom's celebrity worship into absurd territory

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that Josh Groban? Josh Groban is eating the branzino!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I caught that fish. That beautiful fish came from my restaurant, and now Josh Groban is eating it. This is the best day of my life.

jokes.text: Medical Waste Butt-Sweat Grove.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying a more accurate name for Beachview Terrace
jokes.punchline: Extremely gross and specific alternative name
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie tendency to be overly specific and graphic when describing problems

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You know what? Beachview Terrace should really be called Mold Spore Inhalation Pavilion, because that's what you get when you live there.

jokes.text: This place has everything. Vermin, bullet holes, a hornet's nest.
jokes.setup: Real estate agent showing the property
jokes.punchline: Listing terrible features as if they're amenities
jokes.explanation: Standard real estate humor - treating negatives as positives, competent but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Real Estate Agent
dialogue.line: And here we have the master bedroom with its original 1970s wood paneling and a charming water stain that really adds character to the ceiling. The windows are sealed shut to preserve the authentic drafty feel, and that mysterious smell? That's just the bones of the house settling. In the kitchen, you'll notice the appliances are all avocado green—very retro, very in right now. The refrigerator hasn't worked since 2003, but it's a wonderful conversation starter. And this electrical outlet here sparks occasionally, which means the wiring is really alive and doing its job. The bathroom features a toilet that runs constantly—it's like having a personal water fountain! Plus, the mold in the corner gives the tiles a lovely tie-dye effect. The basement floods every spring, but that just means you have a built-in swimming pool. And finally, the foundation has some significant cracks, but they add structural character. So, shall we talk about putting in an offer?

jokes.text: The floor hole.
jokes.setup: Everyone asking where Andy came from
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact reference to emerging from a hole in the floor
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment showing the building's poor condition and Andy's casual acceptance of it

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What? I came up through the hole in the floor. Where did you guys think I came from?

jokes.text: We're losing the cranberry salad, effective immediately!
jokes.setup: Craig briefly becoming boss
jokes.punchline: Immediately making drastic menu changes with his authority
jokes.explanation: Perfect Craig moment - immediately drunk with power and making arbitrary dramatic decisions

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I'm in charge now. First order of business: we're eliminating all the vegetables from the menu.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Second order of business: deep frying everything that's left.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Third order of business: we're calling it 'Sweetums Lite.'

jokes.text: Well, I wish my reign had lasted longer, but I'm happy with the decisions I made and the people I fired.
jokes.setup: Craig's brief time as boss ending
jokes.punchline: His dramatic reflection on his 30-second leadership including firing people
jokes.explanation: Great Craig escalation - treating brief authority like a historical reign and apparently firing people immediately

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I was a boss for 30 seconds. In that time, I fired three people, made two questionable decisions, and one person cried. It was the best 30 seconds of my life.

jokes.text: We wouldn't have to hire Banksy.
jokes.setup: Looking at pre-graffitied buildings
jokes.punchline: Treating existing graffiti as equivalent to famous artist's work
jokes.explanation: Decent tech bro humor showing their disconnect from reality and overvaluing of 'authentic' urban decay

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Executive
dialogue.line: We're treating the existing graffiti as equivalent to famous artist's work.

jokes.text: Okay, but we spent hella skrilla on this trill forest.
jokes.setup: Roscoe explaining their investment in the forest land
jokes.punchline: Uses outdated slang 'hella skrilla' and 'trill' in a business context
jokes.explanation: Good tech bro characterization. The mixing of slang with business speak captures the character type well.

dialogue.speaker: Roscoe
dialogue.line: Look, I invested in this forest land because I saw the potential to make hella skrilla. This deal is trill, and I'm about to be rolling in that green.

jokes.text: Here's a far-out idea that just came to the top of my brainium.
jokes.setup: Andy about to suggest donating the land
jokes.punchline: His made-up word 'brainium' for brain
jokes.explanation: Typical Andy wordplay - childish but endearing made-up word that fits his character

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We should donate the land to the city. It's the right thing to do. I mean, think about it—we'd be using our brainium!

jokes.text: And truthfully, they're all shrooming super hard at Coachella right now.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he needs to consult his board
jokes.punchline: Casual admission his executives are on psychedelic drugs at a music festival
jokes.explanation: Good tech company satire - casual drug use and festival attendance as business obstacles

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Executive
dialogue.line: I'd love to help, but I need to consult with my board first.

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Executive
dialogue.line: They're at Coachella right now on a lot of molly.

jokes.text: I'm watching porn with my right eye.
jokes.setup: Executive mentioning uploading to his smart glasses
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to watching pornography during business meeting
jokes.explanation: Shock humor that fits the tech bro character but feels somewhat cheap and obvious

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Executive
dialogue.line: I can upload directly to my smart glasses.

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl Executive
dialogue.line: Which is great because I was watching pornography during the meeting.

jokes.text: And like that, they were gone.
jokes.setup: Andy's dramatic exit as Jonathan Karate
jokes.punchline: Movie-style dramatic narration of their departure
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his dramatic flair and movie reference knowledge as Jonathan Karate

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: He came to us like a loon in a three-piece suit. We didn't ask for him. We didn't want him. But he was ours.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: And when he left, he took a piece of us with him. Specifically, my good stapler and about forty dollars from petty cash.

jokes.text: Why do we even have a buddy system?
jokes.setup: Andy realizing he's lost track of his ninjas
jokes.punchline: Questioning the safety system while demonstrating why it's needed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Andy's well-meaning but incompetent leadership of children

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, where are my ninjas? I had like... I'm pretty sure I had ninjas.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: How is that possible? We have a whole system in place to keep track of them.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh, I see the problem. The system doesn't work.

jokes.text: You jerk. You sat on that news and let me stew here for three hours?
jokes.setup: Ron reveals he's known about the plan's approval
jokes.punchline: Leslie's outrage at his deliberate withholding of good news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron move - deliberate emotional torture disguised as teaching moment, very in character

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, you knew? You knew the whole time and you didn't tell me?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I wanted to see how long you could go without telling everyone.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ron! That's not funny! I've been stressed out of my mind!

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: It was a little funny.

jokes.text: I do not approve of this. Don't care.
jokes.setup: Ron disapproving of Leslie hugging him
jokes.punchline: Leslie completely ignoring his protests
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending moment showing their relationship dynamic - his boundaries versus her affection

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ron, I'm going to hug you now.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Leslie, no. I don't want—

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Too late!

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Get off me. I'm serious. This is inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You love this and you know it.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I absolutely do not.

tmdb_overview: Leslie holds a rally in an attempt to keep JJ's Diner from closing. Tom surprises Donna with the ultimate pre-wedding gift.
insight: Final season nostalgia carries 67 jokes through a restaurant-saving plot at moderate velocity.
